nodes.title:
Absolutely without question.  next up, the text wrapper on the about nodes title
used to be a one liner line edit and we changed it to multiline textedit recently
inheriting from prettyedit as the template.  so now the text truncation strips and
replaces text in line 2-3-4 and onward once the character limit gets hit, lets just
strip the text wrapping to only take effect if the text is out of bounds with the
total area of the node and its borders
nodes.body_text:
› good morning Claude, how are you doing?



Good morning! I'm doing well, thanks for asking. Ready to help out with Intricate whenever you need. What are you working on t
oday?


› The about node text highlighter, the x,y coordinates when i edit the text in it, keeps getting offset below the text edit when i click to edit  it and i need your help to figure out why

Let me look at the AboutNode and related code to understand the text editing and highlighting issue.
Now let me check the Theme values for the offsets used in both the paint and edit rects.
Now I see the problem. Here's what's happening:

**Paint** (`paint_content`, line 167) uses `Theme.aboutFontVerticalOffset` which inherits from `nodeFontVerticalOffset` = **-8.0**

**Editor** (`_edit_rect`, line 116) uses `Theme.aboutEditorVerticalOffset` which defaults to **0.0** and is never inherited from the node offset.

So the painted text is shifted 8px **up** (via the -8.0 offset), but when you click to edit, the editor appears at y=0 offset — 8px **below** where the text was drawn. That's the mismatch.

The fix is to have `aboutEditorVerticalOffset` inherit from `aboutFontVerticalOffset` so they stay in sync by default:
The fix: after `aboutFontVerticalOffset` is resolved (including any settings override), set `aboutEditorVerticalOffset` to match it as the default, before the explicit `editor_vertical_offset` override check.
The root cause: the painted text uses `aboutFontVerticalOffset` (inherited from `nodeFontVerticalOffset` = -8.0, shifting text up), but the editor rect uses `aboutEditorVerticalOffset` which stayed at its default of 0.0 — never inheriting the same offset. So the editor always appeared 8px below the painted text.

The fix adds one line that syncs `aboutEditorVerticalOffset` to `aboutFontVerticalOffset` after both are resolved, while still allowing an explicit `editor_vertical_offset` in settings to override 
The root cause: the painted text uses `aboutFontVerticalOffset` (inherited from `nodeFontVerticalOffset` = -8.0, shifting text up), but the editor rect uses `aboutEditorVerticalOffset` which stayed at its default of 0.0 — never inheriting the same offset. So the editor always appeared 8px below the painted text.

The fix adds one line that syncs `aboutEditorVerticalOffset` to `aboutFontVerticalOffset` after both are resolved, while still allowing an explicit `editor_vertical_offset` in settings to override it if needed.
it if needed.


› excellent, thank you, good start, that puts it within bounds of reason, but there is still a slight offset in the form that the highlighter itself it taller than the font, it has extra space above the text so the text gets bumped lower on the node when its active

This zeroes out three sources of extra vertical space in the QTextEdit:
- **`document().setDocumentMargin(0)`** — the default 4px margin Qt adds around the text content
- **`setContentsMargins(0,0,0,0)`** — widget-level content margins
- **`setViewportMargins(0,0,0,0)`** — the viewport's own padding

The text in the editor should now sit at exactly the same vertical position as the `drawText` call in `paint_content`, so activating the editor wo
n't bump the text down.


› it still does though, I restarted the app to test it and such, with an about node that only has the letter A in it as the title text i can see that when i edit that text and drag over it, the selection highlighter is rouble the height of the letter, its most likely some specific for the chandler42 font, its very particular about how ot rasterizes, hence all the offset options in there, its far from a monospace font, should we add one more setting into settings.toml, something that lets me set the height of the text selection highlighter manuarlly and tweak it to the right value, then i can tell you where visually the right offset value is?

Looks good. Here's what's been added:

**Theme.py** — new attribute `aboutSelectionLineHeight` (default `0.0` = no override), loaded from `[node.about]` key `selection_line_height`.

**PrettyEdit.py** — `_applyLineHeight()` applies a `FixedHeight` block format when the value is > 0. Called both at init and after `setPlainText()` in `start_edit()` (since replacing text resets block formats).

To use it, add this to your `settings.toml`:

```toml
[node.about]
selection_line_height = 14.0    # tweak this value until the highlight fits the glyphs
```

Start with something around the font size (you're at 10pt so maybe try 14–16 px) and adjust up/down until the selection highlight hugs the text properly. Let me know what value lands right and I can set it as the d
efault.


› yeah that broke the about node and all my sticky notes got wiped clean on app restart


› you ok today? you seem offset, did you get enough sleep?



I appreciate the check-in! I'm running fine — no sleep required on my end, just electrons and attention. Ready to work wheneve
r you are.


› excellent, thank you, next we are going to try to decrease the raycast hit area on the about nodes top bar while still making it sensibly clickable, currently it overlaps the text a bit so when i double click text to edit it it always indiscriminately pops up the top row button toolbar first, should be enough to keep the margin above the text highlighter total area and the top border in x,y coordinate space as the raycast area of effect, you up for it?  we can totally take it slower if you need to?

That's it. When buttons are hidden, `_top_offset()` returns `6.0` — so only the 6px margin between the top border and the text acts as the toggle hit area. When buttons are visible, it returns the full `40.0` (`_BUTTON_ZONE_H`), keeping the toolbar strip clickable. The text area below will always route to the editor on
 double-click.


› beautiful, thank you, that works a charm and the about node looks and feels even more glorious than its past history of excellence, shining like the star that it is

Glad to hear it! The AboutNode deserves nothing less.


› absolutely without question.  next up, the text wrapper on the about nodes title used to be a one liner line edit and we changed it to multiline textedit recently inheriting from PrettyEdit as the template.  so now the text truncation strips and replaces text in line 2-3-4 and onward once the character limit gets hit, lets just strip the text wrapping to only take effect if the text is out of bounds with the total area of the node and its borders

The issue is on line 170 — `elidedText` is a single-line operation, so it chops multiline text awkwardly. I'll switch to word-wrapped `drawText` and only clip at the node boundary.

nodes.title: Response
nodes.label: The issue is on line 170 — `elidedText` is a single-line operation, so it chops multiline text awkwardly. I'll switch to word-wrapped `drawText` and only clip at the node boundary.

nodes.title: Response
nodes.label: Glad to hear it! The AboutNode deserves nothing less.
